items.title: RBI imposes monetary penalty on Mogaveera Co-operative Bank Ltd., Mumbai
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has, by an order dated April 29, 2026, imposed a monetary penalty of ₹20,000/- (Rupees Twenty Thousand only) on Mogaveera Co-operative Bank Ltd., Mumbai (the bank) for non-compliance with certain directions issued by RBI on ‘Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy – Primary (Urban) Co-operative Banks (UCBs)’. This penalty has been imposed in exercise of powers conferred on RBI under the provisions of Section 47A(1)(c) read with Sections 46(4)(i) and 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949. The statutory inspection of the bank was conducted by RBI with reference to i

items.title: RBI reiterates caution against unauthorised and misleading campaigns promising Loan Waivers
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) draws attention of the public to Press Release dated December 11, 2023 cautioning members of the public on false and misleading campaigns promising loan waivers. RBI has observed, with serious concern, the continued occurrence of such campaigns, through various media channels and direct outreach, by certain individuals and entities. Such campaigns not only mislead the general public but also interfere with the orderly functioning of the credit system of the country. 2. These campaigns, among others, involve (i) false promises of waiver of outstanding dues to ban

items.title: RBI imposes monetary penalty on Pandharpur Urban Co-operative Bank Ltd., Pandharpur, Maharashtra
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBl) has, by an order dated April 29, 2026, imposed a monetary penalty of ₹2 lakh (Rupees Two Lakh only) on Pandharpur Urban Co-operative Bank Ltd., Pandharpur, Maharashtra (the bank), for non-compliance with certain directions issued by RBI on ‘Asset classification and treatment of accounts as Non-Performing Assets’. This penalty has been imposed in exercise of powers conferred on RBI under the provisions of Section 47A(1)(c) read with Sections 46(4)(i) and 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949. The statutory inspection of the bank was conducted by the RBI with ref

items.summary: Government of India (GoI) has announced the sale (issue) of one dated security for a notified amount of ₹34,000 crore as per the following details: Sr No Security Date of Repayment Notified Amount (₹ Crore) GoI specific Notification Auction Date Settlement Date 1 New GS 2036 May 11, 2036 34,000 F.No.4(1)-B(W&M)/2026 dated May 04, 2026 May 08, 2026 (Friday) May 11, 2026 (Monday) Total 34,000 2. Yavatmal, Maharashtra, vide Directive No. NGP.DOS.SSM3.No.S611/5-04-166/2025-2026 dated November 06, 2025, for a period of six months up to the close of business on May 07, 2026. 2. Accordingly, the Reserve Bank of India, in exer

items.title: Shri Rohit Jain takes over as RBI Deputy Governor
items.summary: In pursuance of the Government of India notification dated May 02, 2026, Shri Rohit Jain assumed charge as Deputy Governor of Reserve Bank of India today (May 04, 2026) for a period of three years or until further orders, whichever is earlier. Shri Jain was Executive Director of the Reserve Bank before being elevated to the post of Deputy Governor. As Deputy Governor, Shri Jain will look after Department of External Investments and Operations, Financial Markets Regulation Department, Internal Debt Management Department, Foreign Exchange Department, Fintech Department, Risk Monitoring Departmen

items.title: Portfolios of Deputy Governors
items.summary: Consequent on the appointment and assumption of charge by Shri Rohit Jain as Deputy Governor, the distribution of portfolios among the Deputy Governors with effect from May 4, 2026 will be the following: Name Departments Shri Swaminathan Janakiraman* 1. Central Security Cell 2. Consumer Education and Protection Department 3. Department of Supervision 4. Deposit Insurance and Credit Guarantee Corporation 5. Financial Inclusion and Development Department 6. Human Resource Management Department 7. Inspection Department 8. Legal Department 9. Premises Department 10. Right to Information (RIA) Divi

items.title: World Cup prize pool nears $900 million as FIFA boosts payouts. Here’s who gets what
items.summary: FIFA's upcoming World Cup is set to be the most lucrative on-record, with a 'record breaking' prize pool on offer, as FIFA shrugs off other controversies.

items.title: Broker’s Call: Bajaj Finance (Add)
items.summary: InCred Equities

items.title: No government financial support planned for fuel retailers' petrol, diesel, ATF losses
items.summary: The Indian government has declared its stance not to offer financial assistance to its state-run fuel companies, which are currently in the red due to selling fuels like petrol and diesel at a discount. Despite the pressure of escalating crude oil prices, retail costs for these fuels have stayed steady.

items.title: Warsh's take on Fed independence is met with confusion and some concern
items.summary: Fed chair nominee Kevin Warsh is putting his own spin on the independence of monetary policy. Many former Fed officials aren't sold.

items.title: South Korean stocks hit fresh record, building on historic monthly rally in April
items.summary: Asia markets were mixed Monday as investors assess news of President Donald Trump's plan to "free" ships stranded in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: EBay pops as Ryan Cohen says GameStop could issue stock to pay for takeover of much bigger retailer
items.summary: GameStop made a takeover bid for eBay, offering $125 per share in a cash-and-stock deal that values the e-commerce platform at roughly $55.5 billion.

items.title: Broker’s Call: NLC India (Buy)
items.summary: SBI Securities

items.title: Godrej Properties targets ₹39,000 crore in sales for FY27 after record-breaking year
items.summary: In FY26, GPL's sales bookings rose 16% year-on-year to ₹34,171 crore. That was achieved through the sale of 17,513 units spanning 27 million sq ft, the highest ever by an Indian real estate firm.

items.title: Aditya Birla Capital Q4 Results: Net profit rises 31% at Rs 1,129 crore
items.summary: Aditya Birla Capital on Monday reported a 31 per cent growth in consolidated profit at Rs 1,129 crore for the fourth quarter ended March 31 on higher interest income.

items.title: U.S. military denies Iran's claim it struck American warship in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Iran has blocked tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz since the U.S. and Israel started the war in late February, resulting in supply and price shocks.

items.title: Blackstone and Goldman among backers for $1.5bn Anthropic JV
items.summary: New consulting company to advise Wall Street groups on how to deploy its AI across their investment portfolios

items.title: RBI cautions against misleading campaigns promising loan waivers
items.summary: Attention all borrowers! The Reserve Bank of India has flagged a concerning trend involving fraudulent loan waiver initiatives. These duplicitous campaigns claim to erase debts owed to banks and non-banking financial companies (NBFCs) but are merely traps set by dishonest individuals aiming to profit through false promises and upfront fees.

items.title: Wall Street falls on heightened anxieties over Middle East conflict
items.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.17%, the S&P 500 fell 0.02%, the Nasdaq Composite dropped 0.01%

items.title: Small-cap stock under ₹50 hits upper circuit for 13th straight session, declares ₹500 crore fundraise move
items.summary: AVI Polymers shares surged to ₹20.95, driven by a consistent upward trend. The company plans to raise ₹500 crore and expand into technology services. Financial results reveal strong growth, with a net worth increase to ₹115.99 crore by FY26's end.

items.title: NSE launches electronic gold receipts trading
items.summary: The EGRs can be convereted into physical gold by buyers

items.title: Trial of Blackpool’s block trade king begins in Hong Kong
items.summary: Simon Sadler of Segantii Capital faces criminal charges over alleged insider trading of clothing retailer Esprit

items.title: Q4 Results Today Live: Ambuja Cements, Aditya Birla Capital, BHEL, Exide Industries, Petronet, Godrej Properties, Tata Tech Q4 profit rise, Ather Energy narrows loss, Sobha, KEI Ind to announce Q4 results
items.summary: Q4 Results Today, May 4, 2026, Live Updates: Catch live updates

items.title: US stocks today: S&P 500, Dow tick lower as US-Iran tensions unsettle investors
items.summary: Wall Street indexes experienced a slight decline at the market open on Monday. Investor anxiety surrounding the Middle East conflict overshadowed the positive sentiment from last week's earnings reports. The Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq Composite all saw minor drops in early trading.

items.title: Tata Chemicals Q4 results: Cons loss widens YoY to Rs 2,132 crore on exceptional items; revenue drops 2%
items.summary: Tata Chemicals reported a sharp Q4FY26 net loss due to significant impairment charges and weak global pricing. Revenue declined marginally, margins turned negative, and cash flows weakened, though core operations showed improvement before exceptional items amid challenging market conditions.

items.title: CSB Bank Q4 profit rises 6% on lower provisions, growth steady
items.summary: CSB Bank reported 6% Q4 profit growth aided by lower provisions despite higher costs and weaker income. Margins improved, while advances and deposits showed strong growth. Asset quality improved sequentially but weakened year-on-year, reflecting mixed operational performance.

items.title: Solex Energy to invest Rs 4,000 cr to set up solar cell manufacturing plant, BESS project in Gujarat
items.summary: Solex Energy will invest Rs 4,000 crore in Gujarat. The company will establish a solar cell manufacturing facility and a battery energy storage system project. This initiative aims to boost Gujarat's clean-tech manufacturing. It will also create jobs and support India's renewable energy goals. The project includes a 5-GW solar cell plant and a 10-GW BESS project.

items.title: Iran warns US Navy against entering Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Oil surrenders some gains after Washington denies claim that Tehran had struck an American warship

items.title: US denies Iranian report warship was struck by missiles
items.summary: It comes as the US said on Monday it will begin to help "guide" vessels out of the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Tata Technologies Q4 Results: Cons PAT rises 8% YoY to Rs 204 crore, revenue rises 22%
items.summary: Tata Technologies reported 8% year-on-year profit growth in Q4FY26 with strong 22% revenue rise. Improved margins, sequential earnings surge, and dividend announcement highlighted performance, driven by robust services segment growth and operational efficiency.

items.title: Battery raw material lithium may increase further this year
items.summary: However, it will hinge on how the Iran war develops and demand-supply situation

items.title: ADB should step up capital expansion measures, adopt 'more transformational approach': MoS Finance
items.summary: In his address at the ADB Annual Meetings, Pankaj Chaudhary also said that the multilateral funding institution should adopt a "more transformational approach" to support sustainable and resilient regional development amid the current geopolitical challenges.

items.title: European markets fall as Trump threatens new auto tariffs
items.summary: European markets slipped into negative territory Monday, as investors watched Middle East developments.

items.title: Sanjay H Parekh bets big on Reliance, private banks, and domestic plays amid global uncertainty
items.summary: Sohum Asset Managers is shifting its focus to largecap stocks for safety. The fund is overweighting domestic businesses and underweighting global ones. This strategy has benefited performance. Reliance Industries is a key bet, with significant upside expected. Private sector banks also form a large part of the portfolio. The fund is avoiding FMCG due to high valuations.

items.title: ‘Misplaced euphoria’: Markets are sleepwalking into a recession amid Iran war oil price shock
items.summary: As US equities hit fresh highs, strategists warn that investors are downplaying the impact of soaring oil prices.

items.title: EU to water down anti-deforestation rules by exempting leather imports
items.summary: Move to soften impact on companies of flagship climate legislation is a setback for environmental groups

items.title: Thalapathy government? Why Vijay's historic rise in India's industrial powerhouse state matters for stock market
items.summary: Vijay’s TVK is set to reshape Tamil Nadu’s political landscape, prompting investors to assess what a “Thalapathy government” could mean for industry and markets. With the state being a key auto, electronics and export hub, policy clarity on incentives, welfare spending and governance will drive sentiment for TN-linked companies.

items.title: US stock market today: Dow, S&P 500 futures slip as renewed Middle East tensions lift oil prices
items.summary: Investor anxiety surged as US stock futures fluctuated amid reports of a US warship incident near the Strait of Hormuz. While concerns over oil supply disruptions grow, April's robust corporate earnings helped maintain market momentum despite geopolitical risks.

items.title: Carlyle buys majority stake in Knack, EqualizeRCM to build AI-led healthcare billing platform
items.summary: The private equity firm is betting on consolidation in a fragmented US billing services market, with plans to scale via acquisitions and global delivery

items.title: 'Godspeed my friend': Inside the final hours of Spirit Airlines
items.summary: Spirit Airlines ceased operations overnight after a failed government bailout.

items.title: Quote of the day by Walter Schloss: "One of the things you learn in this business is humility because you see your mistakes the next day."
items.summary: Walter Schloss highlights that investing demands humility, as markets quickly expose mistakes through immediate feedback. Rather than avoiding errors, successful investors learn from them—using humility to adapt, cut losses, and refine strategies.

items.title: Bajaj Auto and 4 other stocks hit a 52-week high, rally up to 40% in a month
items.summary: Five BSE 100 stocks, including Adani Power, Adani Ports, Nestle India, Cummins India and Bajaj Auto, hit fresh 52-week highs after rallying up to 40% over the past month.

items.title: US Stock Market Today | Dow Jones | Nasdaq Live: US stocks edge lower as Iran war tensions weigh on sentiment
Why the RBI is weighing a rare 2013-crisis playbook to arrest rupee slide to record lows?
items.summary: The rupee’s slide to fresh record lows and oil above $100 has prompted the RBI to revisit crisis-era tools from 2013, including a special NRI dollar deposit window and possible tax relief for foreign bond investors. With reserves dipping and outflows rising, the central bank is exploring ways to attract fresh dollar inflows.

items.title: Rupee hits record low as Nifty closes well off day’s high
items.summary: Nifty 50 closed at 24,119, up 121 points or 0.51%; Sensex settled at 77,269, adding 356 points or 0.46%

items.title: Citi appoints Raj Rathi as head of mergers & acquisitions in India
items.summary: Rathi joins Citi from Dream Sports, where he served as head of strategy and corporate development.

items.title: Sebi proposes changes to SDI rules to facilitate growth in listed securitisation market
items.summary: Markets regulator Sebi on Monday proposed a series of changes to norms governing securitised debt instruments (SDIs), including allowing single-asset securitisation by RBI-regulated entities, winding up of securitisation transactions and easing certain structural restrictions to boost market development.

items.title: NSE launches Electronic Gold Receipts to formalise India’s gold market; successfully dematerialises 1 kg gold bar
items.summary: The National Stock Exchange introduced Electronic Gold Receipts, enabling dematerialised gold trading backed by physical assets in regulated vaults. The initiative enhances transparency, liquidity, and accessibility, bridging physical gold with financial markets for investors, jewellers, and institutions.

items.title: Central Bank of India expects limited impact from new provisioning rules
items.summary: Central Bank of India managing director and CEO Kalyan Kumar says it is well buffered against the RBI’s shift to stricter, forward-looking loan-loss provisioning, with existing provisions and strong capital expected to absorb any transition impact,

items.title: Supervision must look beyond formal compliance, says RBI Deputy Guv Swaminathan J
items.summary: RBI Deputy Governor Swaminathan J. warns that weak oversight in banking can lead to hidden risks. He stresses that supervision must go beyond reported numbers to understand actual business operations. This approach is crucial for protecting depositors and the wider economy. Digital banking brings new challenges requiring judgment and a sense of public purpose.

items.title: Fertiliser subsidy bill set to overshoot budget estimate of Rs 1.71 lakh cr amid West Asia crisis
items.summary: India's fertiliser subsidy bill for fiscal 2026-27 is set to surpass its budget. Rising costs of imported urea and other fertilisers are driving this increase. Despite global supply chain issues, fertiliser availability for the kharif 2026 season remains strong. Domestic production and imports are being managed to meet demand. States are actively stocking fertilisers in advance.

items.title: Earnings Boom: 10 stocks with over 100% profit growth in Q4FY26
items.summary: ​Several Nifty 500 companies reported over 100% year-on-year net profit growth in Q4FY26, reflecting strong earnings momentum. Firms like Eternal, India Cements, Kajaria Ceramics, and Chennai Petroleum led gains, showcasing improved profitability across sectors including finance, banking, and manufacturing.​

items.title: MRAI asks for dedicated ministry for recycling
items.summary: At the Paryavaran Niti Manthan organised by the Material Recycling Association of India (MRAI) in association with the Niti Aayog on Monday, MRAI has called for called for a central nodal ministry to ensure continuity in leadership, faster decision-making, better inter-ministerial coordination and streamlined compliance processes.

items.title: Wall St futures slip as US-Iran tensions fuel volatility
items.summary: Wall ​Street futures whipsawed on Monday after conflicting reports about a U.S. warship near the Strait of Hormuz heightened investor anxiety over the Middle East conflict.

items.title: TVK poised for Tamil Nadu win: What Thalapathy Vijay’s victory could mean for companies operating in the state
items.summary: Vijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) is set to win the Tamil Nadu Assembly Election 2026. Most exit polls had projected that the DMK-led alliance under Chief Minister M K Stalin would retain power.

items.title: NCLAT sets aside NCLT insolvency order against Embassy Developments
items.summary: Embassy Developments has won a significant legal battle. The National Company Law Appellate Tribunal has cancelled an earlier order that placed the company into insolvency proceedings. This ruling brings an end to the corporate insolvency resolution process. The developer's operations and projects remain unaffected. This positive development follows strong financial performance, with substantial pre-sales reported for the fiscal year.

items.title: Manappuram Finance Q4 Results: Firm swings to black with ₹405 crore profit, announces ₹0.50 dividend
items.summary: Manappuram Finance's Q4 consolidated profit came in at ₹404.79 crore as against a loss of ₹203.17 crore in the same period a year ago. Meanwhile, on a quarter-on-quarter (QoQ) basis, the figure rose 69% from ₹238.55 crore in the December quarter.

items.title: Gainers & Losers: BHEL, DMart among 6 big movers on Monday
items.summary: Indian markets closed higher as election trends boosted sentiment. Stocks such as Valor Estate, BHEL, and Vedanta surged, whereas Sun TV, Avenue Supermarts, and Kotak Bank declined.

items.title: Gold, silver rates today nosedive up to 5% as crude oil price soars after escalation in the US-Iran war
items.summary: US-Iran war: According to the News Agency Reuters, Iran hit two missiles at a US warship on Monday, when it was sailing near Jask island

items.title: Crude oil jumps over 5% as US-Iran tensions flare again; Brent crude climbs past $114
items.summary: Crude oil prices rose on May 4 after a tanker was reportedly hit in the Strait of Hormuz. Tensions escalated between the U.S. and Iran, with Trump promising to guide ships through the strait, while Iran warned of military action against US forces and urged coordination for passing vessels.

items.title: India must map, monitor economic vulnerabilities in energy, food, other areas: EAC-PM chairman
items.summary: Economic Advisory Council to the Prime Minister (EAC-PM) Chairman S Mahendra Dev in an interview with PTI further said the ongoing West Asia conflict highlights the need for a forward-looking approach to risk management.

items.title: Rupee falls 39 paise to end at all-time low of 95.23 against US dollar — What's plaguing the domestic currency?
items.summary: At the interbank foreign exchange market, the rupee opened at 94.95 against the US dollar, then lost ground and finally settled for the day at 95.23 (provisional), registering a fall of 39 paise over its previous close.

items.title: India has no plans for financial support for fuel retailers, official says
items.summary: India will not compensate state-run fuel retailers for losses incurred by selling transport fuels below market prices. While companies have increased prices for industrial customers and jet fuel for foreign carriers, retail prices for gasoline, gasoil, LPG, and jet fuel for Indian carriers remain unchanged. The government prioritizes protecting retail consumers.

items.title: Jindal Stainless Q4 Results: Co posts quarterly profit jump on strong domestic demand
items.summary: Jindal Stainless has seen a significant profit increase of 42.7 percent in its fourth quarter. This growth is attributed to robust domestic demand for its stainless steel products. The company's net revenue also saw a healthy rise. Despite challenges like fuel shortages and shipping delays, Jindal Stainless is expanding its production capacity.

items.title: Zen Technologies shares tumble as Q4 profit and revenue plunge
items.summary: Company posted a standalone net profit of ₹14.75 crore for the quarter ended March 2026, marking a decline of over 83 per cent y-o-y from ₹84.92 crore

items.title: Kotak Mahindra Bank shares fall despite Q4 profit beat, top Nifty 50 loser
items.summary: Brokerages largely acknowledged the earnings beat but offered a mixed outlook

items.title: Sun TV Network falls 9% as DMK trails in Tamil Nadu election trends
items.summary: Market sentiment was also influenced by historical ties between the company’s promoters and the DMK, raising expectations of volatility as counting continues

items.title: Rupee falls 39 paise to close at all-time low of 95.23 against US dollar
items.summary: Crude oil prices at $110 per barrel, foreign capital outflows pressure rupee

items.title: Oil jumps by 5% after report of US warship being hit by missiles
items.summary: Oil prices rose about 5% on Monday after Iran's Fars news agency ​reported an incident with a U.S. ​warship in the Strait of Hormuz, sparking concerns over a prolonged ​disruption in the vital oil transit route.

items.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Highlights: Benchmark indices close firmer; Sensex settles 355 points higher at 77,269, Nifty up 121 points at 24,119
items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Prices Updates: Sensex settles 355.90 points or 0.46 per cent positive at 77,269.40, and Nifty 50 rises by 121.75 points or 0.51 per cent to 24,119.30

items.title: 'Bubble effect': Weight loss drug fueled growth is putting the pharma sector at risk, report finds
items.summary: Obesity assets represent about 25% of total forecast sales of the late-stage pipeline, while oncology's share has slipped to 20%, Deloitte found.

items.title: Rupee sinks to fresh closing low of 95.08 against USD on NDF maturities, firm crude
items.summary: The Indian rupee has reached its weakest point ever. This is due to increased demand for dollars and higher oil prices. India's central bank is exploring ways to attract dollar inflows. These measures aim to strengthen foreign exchange reserves. The rupee faces pressure from rising oil costs linked to the Iran war. Capital outflows have also impacted the currency.

items.title: Is the US truly resilient to the energy market strife?
items.summary: ‘Somewhat counterintuitive’ indeed

items.title: Temasek arm backs Milky Mist ahead of IPO as part of Rs 482 crore pre-listing round
items.summary: Milky Mist Dairy Food has raised Rs 482 crore in a Temasek-backed pre-IPO round, led by Jongsong Investments. The funding strengthens its balance sheet ahead of its planned market debut, signalling strong institutional interest in the value-added dairy brand as it accelerates expansion and prepares for listing.

items.title: GameStop makes $55.5bn takeover offer for eBay
items.summary: GameStop's boss Ryan Cohen says he sees potential to make eBay a much bigger rival to Amazon.

items.title: IndiGo's Head of Global Sales Vinay Malhotra resigns
items.summary: Vinay Malhotra, IndiGo's Head of Global Sales, has resigned. He will leave his position on July 3. Malhotra is seeking new opportunities outside the airline. This follows recent leadership changes at the country's largest airline. IndiGo previously announced William Walsh as its new CEO and Aloke Singh as Chief Strategy Officer.

items.title: ET Alpha Wealth Summit: D-St moguls set to decode the next wealth-creation avenues
items.summary: The Economic Times will host the ET Alpha Wealth Summit on June 4 in Mumbai. Fund managers and market veterans like Saurabh Mukherjea, S Naren, Nilesh Shah, Vikash Khemani, Rajeev Thakkar and Rajesh Saluja will discuss future wealth creation avenues. Sessions will explore identifying opportunities in a market with fewer cheap stocks.

items.title: Milky Mist raises ₹482 crore in pre-IPO round led by Temasek arm
items.summary: The entire primary investment was led by Jongsong Investments, while promoters Sathishkumar T. and Anitha S. sold 89,43,903 equity shares at ₹139.76 a share, aggregating roughly ₹125 crore, as part of a secondary transaction.

items.title: Top Gainers & Losers on May 4: Meesho, Vedanta, HFCL, BHEL, Cemindia Projects, Anant Raj among top gainers
items.summary: On May 4, major indices saw gains due to strong buying in heavyweight stocks and positive state election trends for the Bharatiya Janata Party. The Nifty 50 rose 0.51% to 24,119, while the S&P BSE Sensex increased 0.31% to 77,159, with midcap and smallcap indices outperforming.

items.title: MahaRERA cracks down on errant builders; issues notices over 8,212 projects
items.summary: MahaRERA is taking strict action against over 8,000 housing projects. These developers failed to submit mandatory quarterly progress reports on time. This includes many projects in the Mumbai Metropolitan Region. MahaRERA has issued show-cause notices. Developers have 60 days to comply. Failure could lead to severe penalties, including project registration cancellation.

items.title: Election impact on stock market explained: What likely BJP win in West Bengal means for investors
items.summary: West Bengal's election results signal a significant political shift with the BJP leading, prompting a surge in market sentiment. Analysts anticipate gradual improvements and long-term benefits for sectors like real estate and infrastructure, aligning with the BJP's manifesto. However, global macro indicators remain key drivers for Indian equities.

items.title: GE Vernova gets order to supply 9 pumped storage units in India
items.summary: GE Vernova will supply nine pumped-storage units for Megha Engineering & Infrastructures Limited's Upper Sileru hydropower plant in Andhra Pradesh. This 1.35-gigawatt project, expected by 2030, will be one of India's largest. Pumped storage is vital for grid stability and balancing renewable energy. GE Vernova's work includes design, manufacturing, and commissioning of the units and control systems.

items.title: Canada’s beef producers say Mercosur trade deal will devastate industry
items.summary: Farmers fear a flood of cheap meat as Carney government looks for entry to South American bloc

items.title: The US decoupling from the global gloom has limits
items.summary: Total economic and financial insulation is an impossibility

items.title: Commodity Radar: As gold holds near Rs 1.5 lakh support, NFP data to decide next breakout. Buy-on-dips strategy emerges
items.summary: Gold prices are consolidating near key support levels around Rs 1.5 lakh after a brief correction, with analysts recommending a buy-on-dips strategy. Market direction hinges on upcoming US Nonfarm Payrolls data, which could influence dollar strength and interest rate expectations, thereby determining gold’s next breakout or continued range-bound movement.

items.title: Sebi-backed PaRRVA mechanism for performance validation becomes operational. Check details
items.summary: CARE Ratings Limited and National Stock Exchange of India have launched PaRRVA, a framework to verify performance claims in the securities market. Conceptualised by Securities and Exchange Board of India, the platform aims to independently validate risk-return data and standardise disclosures for investors as market participation grows.

items.title: Carlyle acquires Knack RCM and Equalize RCM to create an AI-native, global multi-specialty healthcare RCM platform
items.summary: Global investment firm Carlyle has acquired a majority stake in US healthcare revenue cycle management providers Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM. This move aims to create a global, AI-native, multi-specialty RCM platform. The combined entity will leverage AI and global delivery to enhance operational scale and client outcomes, addressing critical financial risks for healthcare providers.

items.title: Baazar Style, Senco Gold, other stocks jump up to 9% on BJP's early lead in West Bengal elections. Here's why
items.summary: Shares of Kolkata-based companies surged after early trends in the West Bengal Assembly elections showed a strong lead for the BJP. Stocks like Baazar Style Retail, Senco Gold and CESC rallied on expectations of higher consumption and economic activity, although analysts caution that the market impact may remain short term.

items.title: Silver futures plunge Rs 3,917 to Rs 2.47 lakh/kg on weak global trends
items.summary: Silver prices plunged Rs 3,917 to Rs 2.47 lakh per kilogram in futures trade on Monday as elevated crude oil rates amid inflation concerns dampened investors sentiment.

items.title: FPI outflows persist in equities; primary market sees continued interest
items.summary: In 2026, IPO activity is subdued with few listings, while Foreign Portfolio Investors (FPIs) invested ₹12,339 crore in the primary market. Despite significant outflows from equities, FPIs are attracted to primary issues due to appealing valuations and yield prospects.

items.title: India's LPG consumption slides 16 pc in April amid West Asia conflict
items.summary: Cooking gas LPG use in India saw a significant 16 percent drop in April. Supply issues stemming from the West Asia conflict impacted availability for homes and businesses. This led to reduced consumption compared to last year and the previous month. Other fuel sales like diesel and petrol also showed moderated growth.

items.title: BSE SME stock rebounds after announcement of H2 results 2026
items.summary: Harshdeep Hortico Limited's stock rose 9% to ₹100 following positive H2FY26 results, with revenue increasing nearly 15% year-on-year. The company reported improved EBITDA and a 25% rise in net profit, reflecting strong financial performance.

items.title: Gold, silver prices outlook: Where are bullion prices headed this month?
items.summary: Gold, Silver prices outlook: Persistent inflation and expectations of delayed interest rate cuts may cap the upside in gold prices in the near term. However, ongoing geopolitical tensions and steady physical demand are likely to provide underlying support.

items.title: Bank Nifty falls 8% in 2026 so far: Is this correction a buying opportunity?
items.summary: As Bank Nifty faces an 8% decline in 2026, experts debate whether this downturn presents a buying opportunity for investors. While some advise caution, others highlight the attractive entry points for select banking stocks amidst ongoing market volatility and regulatory changes.

items.title: Treasury yields edge higher as traders monitor factory data and Middle East developments
items.summary: Treasury yields gained Monday as traders looked out for more U.S. factory data, while monitoring developments in the Middle East.

items.title: Gold futures slide to ₹1.50 lakh/10g as oil surge, dollar weigh on bullion
items.summary: During the previous week, gold had depreciated by ₹1,347, or nearly 1 per cent, to close at ₹1.51 lakh per 10 grams

items.title: WeWork India opens new coworking centre in Delhi with over 1,400 desks
items.summary: WeWork India has launched a new co-working space in Aerocity, New Delhi. This expansion adds 1.1 lakh sq ft and over 1,400 desks to its operations. The company is growing to meet the increasing demand for flexible workspaces. This marks WeWork India's 17th centre in the Delhi-NCR region. The company has a significant presence across eight cities in India.

items.title: Global Markets | European shares inch lower as tariff threats drag automakers; investors assess Mideast progress
items.summary: European shares ​ticked lower on Monday as European automakers came under pressure following threats of a U.S. tariff hike, while investors awaited signs of progress in reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Centre plans 3.25 mn ha shift to chemical-free farming by FY31
items.summary: The department of expenditure in coordination with the agriculture ministry, is drafting a proposal to create 65,000 clusters of 50 hectares each under the National Mission on Natural Farming

items.title: Ambuja Cement Q4 results: Net profit rises 78% YoY to ₹1,830 crore; ₹2 per share dividend announced
items.summary: Ambuja Cement Q4 results: Net profit rises 78% YoY to ₹1,830 crore. Ambuja Cement share price was marginally up, rose over 0.39% to ₹445 apiece on NSE.

items.title: Repertoire drinking spurs new spirit launches
items.summary: Nearly a dozen products are either already in market or being introduced through early 2026-27 across whisky, brandy, vodka, gin, rum and tequila.

items.title: Capri Global Capital targets ₹55,000 crore AUM by FY28; gold loans and co-lending drive profitability: Rajesh Sharma
items.summary: Capri Global Capital is targeting ₹55,000 crore in assets under management by FY28. The non-banking financial company is focusing on growth through gold loans and co-lending arrangements. This strategy aims to boost fee income while maintaining profitability. The company plans significant expansion in its gold loan branch network over the next three years.

items.title: April manufacturing PMI: is the uptick temporary?
items.summary: The manufacturing PMI recovered mildly in April to 54.7 from a four-year low, driven partly by pre-emptive inventory building ahead of price hikes rather than genuine demand.

items.title: Value 360 Communications IPO day 1: Issue subscribed 3% so far. Check GMP, status and other key details
items.summary: The shares of Value Communications IPO are commanding a premium of ₹0 in the grey market. This means that the GMP of Value Communications IPO is +0.

items.title: Ambuja Cements Q4 Results: Cons profit surges 78% YoY to Rs 1,830 crore; revenue rises 10%
items.summary: Ambuja Cements Q4 results: Ambuja Cements reported a strong Q4FY26 performance with net profit rising 78% YoY to Rs 1,830 crore and revenue up 10% to its highest-ever quarterly level. The company posted record sales volumes, improved margins, and remained debt-free, even as cost pressures from fuel, logistics and currency depreciation persisted.

items.title: MCX shares drop 3% after Sebi chief's comments on commodity derivatives. What spooked investors?
items.summary: MCX shares fell after SEBI signalled that banks and insurers are unlikely to be allowed into commodity derivatives. SEBI chief Tuhin Kanta Pandey said the regulator won’t push the proposal with Reserve Bank of India.

items.title: Himadri Speciality shares jump 6% to hit a 52-week high after a 38% rise in April amid focus on revenue growth
items.summary: Himadri Speciality Chemicals shares surged 6% to reach a 52-week high, driven by a 38% increase in April. With plans to double profits by FY28 and expand into high-margin segments, the company is set to redefine its market position and reduce India's import dependence in key chemical sectors.

items.title: India palm oil imports fall to one-year low in April on weak demand
items.summary: India's palm oil imports plummeted 27% in April to a one-year low, driven by sluggish institutional demand and a reduced price advantage over rival oils. This decline could impact global palm oil futures, while imports of soyoil and sunflower oil saw significant increases, pushing overall edible oil imports to a 2026 high.

items.title: Hero MotoCorp appoints Sachin Agrawal as CTO
items.summary: Hero MotoCorp has appointed Sachin Agrawal as its new Chief Technology Officer, effective from May 21. Agrawal will lead the company's research and development transformation. He brings over 29 years of experience in automotive R&D and technology leadership. Agrawal previously worked at VE Commercial Vehicles and began his career at Daewoo Motors.

items.title: Aluminium up, cement down: geopolitics drives sector rotation
items.summary: Energy costs and supply shocks drive a divergence between global metals and domestic plays, with analysts flagging a potential reversal if tensions ease.

items.title: India explores steps to mobilise dollar inflows as rupee slides, sources say
items.summary: India's central bank is actively seeking methods to attract dollar inflows. This move aims to bolster foreign exchange reserves and stabilize the rupee. Measures under consideration include reviving a deposit scheme for non-resident Indians and removing withholding tax on overseas government bond investors. These steps are being explored amid rising oil prices and capital outflows.

items.title: How Navi is sharpening its IPO pitch ahead of a fresh FY27 filing
items.summary: Navi Ltd plans to file for an IPO in FY27, focusing on profitability and a diversified business model. The fintech aims to attract investors with its offerings in payments, lending, insurance, and investments.

items.title: Vodafone Idea, HFCL, Meesho, JP Power, BHEL among the most traded stocks today, 4 May 2026
items.summary: Most traded stocks today: Vodafone Idea, HFCL, Meesho, Jaiprakash Power Ventures (JP Power), Bharat Heavy Electricals (BHEL), and Vedanta were the most traded stocks, or the most active stocks in terms of volume, on the NSE.

items.title: Godrej Properties posts record ₹1,850 crore profit in FY26, stock gains
items.summary: Q4 FY26 saw ₹7,947 crore in collections and a 70% jump in net profit to ₹650 crore

items.title: Sebi urges GST Council to fix tax issues in commodity derivatives
items.summary: The regulator has also proposed an integrated GST mechanism for physically delivered commodity derivatives.

items.title: Gold edges lower on rate fears, Iran tensions cap downside
items.summary: MCX Gold is currently trading near ₹1,51,000 per 10 grams, pulling back from a recent multi-week high of ₹1,55,000

items.title: Gains narrow by noon; Kotak Bank, Bharti Airtel drag as broader market stays firm
items.summary: Among Nifty 50 gainers, Hindustan Unilever led with a 3.87% rise to ₹2,338, touching a high of ₹2,365.80, on volumes of 15,12,837 shares worth ₹35,368.01 lakhs

items.title: Why Avenue Supermarts may find it challenging to maintain margin
items.summary: After dropping 5% on Monday following Q4 results, Avenue Supermarts stock is still up around 15% so far in 2026. The stock will take cues from near-term growth rates.

items.title: Hindustan Laboratories, RK Steel get SEBI nod for IPOs
items.summary: Both companies had filed their draft papers in September and received SEBI’s observations on April 27, which effectively clears them to proceed.

items.title: ₹2.75 to ₹151.50: Multibagger penny stock turns ₹1 lakh into ₹55 lakh in four years
items.summary: Magnus Steel and Infra share price, which was once priced at ₹2.77 apiece in May 2022, is now trading at ₹151.50 on the BSE. Here's the share price trend and investment growth of the multibagger penny stock.

items.title: Bajaj Auto total sales up 40% at 5,13,792 units in April
items.summary: Bajaj Auto has announced a significant sales surge for April. Total sales climbed by 40 percent, reaching over 5.13 lakh units. This impressive growth was driven by a substantial 83 percent increase in exports. Domestic sales also saw a healthy rise of 13 percent. The company's two-wheeler segment performed strongly, with both domestic and export figures showing robust gains.

items.title: Ather Energy posts record volumes, narrows losses in FY26; stock slips on results day
items.summary: FY26 total income reached ₹3,823 crore, a 66% y-o-y increase and the company’s highest annual revenue; Q4 FY26 revenue was ₹1,214 crore, up 76% y-o-y

items.title: Tamil Nadu election results impact: Why have Sun TV Network shares crashed over 9% today?
items.summary: Sun TV, part of the Sun Group, is led by Indian billionaire media proprietor Kalanithi Maran — who is closely related to DMK leaders. Maran is the grandnephew of DMK president M Karunanidhi and nephew of the current chief minister MK Stalin.

items.title: RBI, IRDAI not inclined to allow commodity derivative investments, SEBI says
items.summary: Shares of Multi Commodity Exchange of India(MCX), India's first listed exchange, fell 3.4%

items.title: Tactical tool or red flag? Inside the Q4 promoter pledging churn
items.summary: Moderating pledge levels give way to resurfacing stress as market corrections expose underlying promoter debt

items.title: CNBC Daily Open: As Gulf fatigue sets in, earnings take center stage
items.summary: As geopolitical developments indicate a stalemate, company earnings have taken center stage.

items.title: Govt has eased FDI norms for foreign cos having small Chinese/Hong Kong stake: An explainer
items.summary: India has eased Foreign Direct Investment rules for companies with up to ten percent Chinese or Hong Kong shareholding. These firms can now invest in India through the automatic route starting May 1, 2026. This change aims to boost investment flows. Previously, stricter rules applied due to security concerns. The government has also expedited clearances for certain manufacturing sectors.

items.title: Our pension funds are not quasi-government money
items.summary: New legislation pushing the mandatory allocation of investments into private assets conflicts with the fiduciary duty of trustees

items.title: Prudent Corporate launches AI platform ‘Edge+’ for mutual fund distributors
items.summary: The platform integrates capabilities such as goal-based planning, business analytics, marketing automation and research tools under a single interface

items.title: WeWork India opens new Aerocity centre, expands NCR footprint
items.summary: The facility targets enterprises, GCCs, and high-growth firms with premium amenities and strong connectivity

items.title: 1,400% returns in five years! Multibagger small-cap stock Spice Lounge hits upper circuit
items.summary: Spice Lounge Food Works share price has fallen 23% in one month and has declined 28% in three months. The smallcap has dropped 46% on a year-to-date (YTD) basis, while it has plunged 63% in six months.

items.title: Vi shares jump on AGR relief; dues cut by ₹23,649 crore
items.summary: Under the revised payment schedule, Vi will pay ₹124 crore annually from March 2026 through March 2031, covering dues pertaining to FY18 and FY19 that were outside the scope of the reassessment

items.title: Markets open higher; Maruti, Bajaj Auto lead gains as Kotak Bank slides 4%
items.summary: Sensex opened at 77,257.27 and rose to 77,477.48, up 563.98 points (0.73%) shortly after 9:16 am; Nifty opened at 24,063.55 and increased to 24,177.00, gaining 179.45 points (0.75%)

items.title: India is burning more coal as extreme heat and the Iran war squeeze energy supplies
items.summary: India, the world's third-largest emitter of carbon dioxide, is burning more coal as demand for power rises, most of which is coal-fired.

items.title: Vinay Rajani of HDFC Sec suggests Tata Steel, Linde India shares to buy
items.summary: The domestic equity benchmarks rallied on May 4, supported by positive auto stock performance and a dip in crude prices. Nifty 50 increased by 1% to 24,236, indicating a consolidation phase as it faces resistance levels, while broader markets may need time to stabilize.

items.title: Steel Exchange raises ₹40.32 crore via warrants, shares gain on NSE
items.summary: The funds will support expansion, working capital, and strategic initiatives

items.title: 'I had to pay £14k after my cat was run over'
items.summary: People with and without pet insurance are facing large bills when their animals get sick or injured.

items.title: ideaForge shares hit 52-week high as Q4 results trigger surge
items.summary: The rally follows ideaForge’s Q4 FY26 results, in which the company posted its highest-ever quarterly revenue of ₹141.04 crore, up sharply from ₹20.31 crore in Q4 FY25

items.title: BHEL Q4 results 2026: Profit jumps nearly threefold to ₹1,290 crore; declares a final dividend of ₹1.40 for FY26
items.summary: BHEL Q4 results 2026: Profit jumped 156% year-on-year, or nearly threefold (2.6 times), to ₹1,290.47 crore. Revenue from operations for Q4FY26 saw a 37% YoY jump to ₹12,310.37 crore.

items.title: India's factory growth stays sluggish in April amid war-led soaring costs, PMI shows
items.summary: India's manufacturing growth saw a slight uptick in April, reaching 54.7, but remained near a four-year low due to weak domestic demand and rising input costs. Despite challenges, export orders surged, and companies expanded hiring and expressed optimism for future growth.

items.title: Indian private sector new order growth quickens in April but remains slowest in 4 years: PMI survey shows
items.summary: After losing some momentum in March due to West Asia conflict, growth across India's private sector expanded at a quicker rate at the start of new fiscal year in April.

items.title: NCLAT rejects Vedanta's challenge to approval of Adani's resolution plan for Jaiprakash Associates
items.summary: Vedanta's challenge to Adani Enterprises' resolution plan for Jaiprakash Associates Ltd was dismissed by the National Company Law Appellate Tribunal. The tribunal found no legal flaws in the creditors' choice, marking a significant setback for Vedanta despite its claims of offering a superior bid.

items.title: Small-cap pharma stock under ₹50 jumps 4% following Dalal Street rally
items.summary: Sudarshan Pharma Industries saw a 4% stock price increase after reporting a 44.8% rise in Q4 net profit to ₹10.73 crore and a 36.72% revenue growth to ₹220.92 crore. For the year, net profit rose 55.64% and sales climbed 39.21%.

items.title: OPEC+ announces 188,000 barrels-per-day output increase in first meeting without UAE
items.summary: Concerns around production were amplified further last week with news of the UAE's shock departure.

items.title: Indore to host meeting of BRICS agriculture ministers in June
items.summary: Indore will host BRICS agriculture ministers from June 12 to 13. Officials will meet earlier from June 9 to 11. Discussions will focus on food security, smart agriculture, global trade, and farmer welfare.

items.title: Indian bonds gain as higher yields lure value investors; states' poll results eyed
items.summary: The benchmark Indian 6.48% 2035 bond ​yield was at 6.9902%, as of 10:05 ⁠a.m. IST, after closing at 7.0148% on Thursday

items.title: The nuclear option: Atomic energy could offer Europe hope, say analysts — but it won't be easy
items.summary: The effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a vital shipping route, has exposed which nations are most exposed to disrupted energy imports.

items.title: DMart shares tumble 4% after Q4 results, wipe off ₹9,300 crore from Radhakishan Damani & other promoters' wealth
items.summary: Radhakishan Damani and other promoters of the company who cumulatively hold a 74.51% stake in the company, lost ₹9332 crore in notional wealth within minutes amid the decline in the counter.

items.summary: At the interbank foreign exchange market, the rupee touched 94.95 against the US dollar in initial trade, registering a fall of 11 paise over its previous close

items.summary: On the MCX, gold futures fell ₹1,347 (nearly 1%) to ₹1.51 lakh per 10 grams, while silver gained ₹879 to close at ₹2.50 lakh per kilogram last week

items.title: When survival is at stake, Bollywood production houses turn to distribution
items.summary: Bollywood production houses are increasingly entering the film distribution space over the past two years as a structural shift driven by rising production costs and changing revenue models.

items.summary: At 10 am on Monday, July Brent oil futures were $108.18 (+0.01%), and June WTI crude oil futures were $101.63 (-0.30%)

items.title: India resumes wheat exports after four years but high prices likely to limit demand
items.summary: India has resumed wheat exports for the first time in four years, with ITC loading 22,000 metric tons for the UAE. A record harvest and higher global prices and freight rates are making Indian wheat competitive. This follows a ban imposed due to crop damage from extreme heat in previous years.

items.title: Samsung family pays off record $8bn inheritance tax bill
items.summary: The bill is tied to the estate left by the firm's late chairman Lee Kun-hee, who died in October 2020.

items.summary: New Mexico seeks child safety restrictions on Meta apps and algorithms in trial's 2nd phase

items.title: 15 stocks including Infosys, Tech Mahindra, HCLTech, Reliance to remain in focus on May 4
items.summary: India Inc. witnessed brisk dealmaking and expansion, with HCLTech’s AI push, Tech Mahindra’s overseas acquisition, Reliance Retail’s brand buyout, and multiple firms securing orders, appointments, and investments across telecom, energy, pharma, and hospitality

items.title: India is blocking a cheap way to beat the heat wave, one tweak in AC can lower costs
items.summary: India faces a cooling crisis as soaring temperatures drive unprecedented electricity demand. While AC sales surge, a safer, cheaper, and more efficient refrigerant, propane (R290), is being held back by outdated regulations. This overlooked solution could significantly ease grid pressure and reduce climate impact, yet its adoption is inexplicably slow.

items.title: UAE fertiliser giant resorts to trucks to shift product out of Gulf
items.summary: Abu Dhabi’s Fertiglobe switches to land cargo to avoid Strait of Hormuz as soaring prices allow it to shoulder extra costs

items.title: GameStop makes staggering $55.5 billion play to acquire online auction giant eBay
items.summary: GameStop has proposed a $55.5 billion acquisition of eBay, valuing it at $125.00 per share. GameStop CEO Ryan Cohen says he plans to lead the combined entity

items.title: Oil prices subdued after Trump says US plans to help stranded ships cross Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Oil prices held near $108 as Trump announced ‘Project Freedom’ to guide stranded ships through the Strait of Hormuz, though blockade risks and fragile peace prospects kept markets on edge.

items.title: Trump says U.S. will 'free' ships trapped in Persian Gulf by Strait of Hormuz closure
items.summary: Vessels have been largely unable to pass through the Strait of Hormuz for months, disrupting key supply chains.

items.title: Rudy Giuliani, former New York mayor and Trump lawyer, hospitalized in critical condition
items.summary: President Donald Trump called Rudy Giuliani a "True Warrior, and the Best Mayor in the History of New York City, BY FAR," in a Truth Social post.

items.title: Rural job demand under MGNREGS falls by over a third in April
items.summary: The decline in rural work demand in April may be a continuation of similar declines seen over the past few months, though it could also reflect implementation challenges with the VB G-RAM-G.

items.title: Indian equities deserve higher FII allocation, but valuations stretched
items.summary: India merits a higher allocation given its structural strengths. While the underlying business is strong, the valuations are not attractive, said Ramneek Kundra, chief investment officer at DSP Pension Fund Managers. But India’s premium to emerging markets has come down.

items.title: A key moment at Tata Trusts as it votes on the reappointment of two vice chairmen at its wealthiest affiliate
items.summary: An important fallout of the vote will be whether both Srinivasan and Singh can continue as vice-chairmen of Tata Trusts; an adverse outcome would prevent them from holding the titles, as the chairman and vice-chairmen must be trustees of all Tata Trusts.

items.title: EVs moving at faster speed in premium lane
items.summary: India's electric car market is seeing growth in premium segments. Carmakers are launching more expensive models, with sales in the ₹20-30 lakh bracket rising significantly. The share of cars below ₹10 lakh has decreased. Luxury EV demand is also strong. This trend suggests the market is expanding upwards, potentially limiting mass adoption.

items.title: White Whale bets on Blue Tokai, Atomberg via secondaries fund
items.summary: The Mumbai-based venture capital firm, which also backs startups such as M2P Fintech and iD Fresh, has completed the first close of the fund and expects to reach the final close within the next 12-18 months, co-founder Shapath Parikh told Mint.

items.title: Oil slips after Trump says US will help free ships stranded in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Oil prices fell on Monday after President Trump announced efforts to free stranded ships in the Strait of Hormuz, yet remained above $100 due to ongoing U.S.-Iran tensions. Brent crude dropped to $107.53, while U.S. West Texas Intermediate fell to $101.10 amid stalled peace negotiations.

items.title: How Infosys is adapting as GCCs move outsourcing work in-house
items.summary: The Bengaluru-based IT major has mandated dedicated GCC offerings with embedded AI capabilities across its delivery centres, while also setting aside capacity within its campuses to incubate client-owned tech hubs.

items.title: Pirro reveals new Trump attack evidence; Cole Allen removed from 'suicide precautions'
items.summary: Until U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro's comments, prosecutors had not disclosed whose bullet hit the agent.

items.title: Amsterdam bans public adverts for meat and fossil fuels
items.summary: Local politicians say the move is in line with the Dutch capital's environmental targets.

items.title: The threat to summer holidays looming from jet fuel shortages
items.summary: What impact might shortages have on our summer holidays - and what could be done about it?

items.title: Greg Abel earns solid scorecard from Berkshire shareholders after first annual meeting
items.summary: The reviews from longtime shareholders and professional investors were broadly positive, even as many acknowledged the notable absence of Warren Buffett.

items.title: In an Age of Doom Scrolling, Asia’s Book Market Is Thriving
items.summary: The internet was supposed to kill off books. But across much of Asia, a booming market tells a different story.

items.title: Third party premiums take the wheel in motor cover growth
items.summary: TP premiums rose 9.3% in FY26, compared with 9% growth in OD, taking overall motor insurance expansion to about 9% on an estimated base of ?1.08 lakh crore. This contrasts with FY24, when OD grew a stronger 17.4% against roughly 10% for TP, supported by robust vehicle sales and pricing improvements. In FY25, OD grew at 8.1% while TP grew at 7.8%. The latest shift comes despite no increase in regulated TP premium rates since the pandemic.

items.title: Public sector banks rush to build up wealth management assets
items.summary: The move is aimed at improving customer engagement as household savings shift from bank deposits to market-linked instruments and risk products. Indian Bank plans to set up a wealth management vertical targeting high net worth individuals, while sectoral leader State Bank of India aims to grow its wealth assets under management fivefold to Rs 15 lakh crore by 2030.

items.title: Tax shadow over charitable trusts with 'profit' motive
items.summary: Three top-notch hospitals in Mumbai and a famous international spiritual organisation-all functioning as charitable trusts-have been denied Income tax (I-T) exemption on the grounds that they were carrying out commercial activities.

items.title: Nestlé India flags Iran war risks, holds prices amid cost pressures
items.summary: Nestle India faces economic challenges including the Iran war, inflation, and a weak monsoon prediction. The company is increasing advertising, technology, and capital expenditure to manage these uncertainties. Despite these factors, Nestle India reported strong growth in the March quarter. The company aims for volume-led growth in its core categories.

items.title: Vodafone Idea eyes ₹25,000 crore SBI-led funding after AGR relief
items.summary: A State Bank of India led consortium is looking at providing ₹25,000 crore debt funding to Vodafone Idea. This comes after the government significantly reduced the telco's dues. Vodafone Idea is also seeking ₹10,000 crore for working capital. The company plans a three-year turnaround program focusing on network upgrades and 5G expansion.

items.title: Saint-Gobain to invest $1 billion in India, bets on growth shift
items.summary: French company Saint-Gobain will invest nearly one billion dollars in India over the next five years. This investment signals a major shift towards emerging markets for growth. India's growing population and urbanisation are key drivers. Saint-Gobain aims to triple its business in India within ten years. The company is expanding its presence in various infrastructure projects across the country.

items.title: MSME apprenticeships set for boost as India drafts roadmap
items.summary: India is embarking on an innovative strategy to enhance apprenticeship programs tailored for micro, small, and medium enterprises. Focused reforms in policies, regulations, and financial frameworks are on the table, with a group training model being a key contender. The goal is to equip the nation's youth with essential workforce skills by 2047.

items.title: HSN classification muddle raises costs for battery storage sector
items.summary: The industry has sought separate HSN codes, an approved manufacturer list and tighter local content norms, along with clarity on customs duty deferment under warehouse rules for the 12-year life of storage projects.

items.title: Govt says auto fuel shortage rumour led to panic buys, assures stocks adequate
items.summary: While cooking gas supplies remained strained due to the West Asia war, household demand had been being prioritized and no shortages have been reported, the petroleum ministry said.

items.title: OPEC members increase oil production quota by 188,000 bpd, no word on UAE's withdrawal amid war in West Asia
items.summary: Saudi Arabia, Russia, and five other OPEC members increased their oil production quota by 188,000 barrels per day, despite the UAE's recent withdrawal. Analysts suggest this move aims to project stability amid ongoing market disruptions from the Mideast conflict.

items.title: UK government plans to allow airlines to consolidate flights as jet fuel costs soar
items.summary: The government says it wants to reduce the likelihood of last-minute flight cancelations over the summer amid uncertainty over the Iran war.

items.title: New policy will benefit premium brands, hurt local ones, distillers tell Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah
items.summary: Local alcohol manufacturers in Karnataka have urged Chief Minister Siddaramaiah to review the proposed liquor tax system, saying it favours multinational premium brands over cheaper local products.

items.title: Air India board meeting on May 7; cost-saving steps, CEO selection likely on agenda
items.summary: Air India's board meets May 7 to discuss cost cuts and a new CEO. The airline faces significant losses and challenges from rising jet fuel prices and West Asia conflict. Plans include unbundling meals and lounge access. The search for a new CEO is underway. These measures aim to navigate a difficult period for the airline.

items.title: UAE oil giant ADNOC pledges $55 billion in new projects by 2028: statement
items.summary: The Abu Dhabi National Oil Company on Sunday pledged to spend $55 billion on new projects over the next two years, just days after the United Arab Emirates' officially left the OPEC oil cartel. The UAE has been an OPEC member through the emirate of Abu Dhabi since 1967, four years before the former British protectorate became an independent country.

items.title: Delhi Metro expansion: CM Rekha Gupta to unveil 97 km plan under Phase V(b)
items.summary: Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta is set to announce Phase V (b) of the Delhi Metro, a 97-km expansion across seven corridors with 65 stations. This ambitious project, costing over Rs 48,000 crore, aims to extend the network into outer city areas, enhancing connectivity and promoting sustainable transport. Four priority corridors are targeted for completion by 2029.

items.title: Dharavi Redevelopment: Upper-floor home rehab hinges on ground-floor eligibility
items.summary: Upper-floor residents in the Dharavi redevelopment project may face eligibility challenges, as their rehabilitation depends partly on whether the ground-floor occupant qualifies. Under a Maharashtra government resolution, they can get 300 sq ft homes in the Mumbai Metropolitan Region if they prove occupancy before November 15, 2022 — marking the first inclusion of upper-floor residents in such schemes.

items.title: In five charts: How UAE's exit could affect Opec's influence over the oil price
items.summary: The BBC takes a look in charts at what the UAE's departure could mean for the oil cartel and more widely.

items.title: Nestle India looks for volume-led growth despite price volatility amid geopolitical uncertainties
items.summary: Nestle India aims for volume-led growth in the new fiscal year. The company is cautious about price volatility stemming from rising input costs and geopolitical tensions. Nestle India will continue to invest in its brands and expand its rural reach. The company is also open to strategic acquisitions to strengthen its portfolio.

items.title: There is method in the market’s madness
items.summary: But it relies on a host of rosy assumptions going in investors’ favour

items.title: Two separate venues for pharma industry expos in Delhi-NCR
items.summary: CPHI India and PMEC India are expanding their pharmaceutical expos to two venues in Delhi-NCR. This move aims to support the rapid growth of India's pharma sector. The events will offer greater business engagement and specialized networking. CPHI India will focus on ingredients and formulations. PMEC India will showcase machinery and manufacturing technologies.

items.title: The silent treatment: Saudi Arabia’s long game for managing Opec
items.summary: Middle East conflict forces Riyadh to postpone any response to the UAE’s decision to quit the oil cartel

items.title: Trump’s green new deal
items.summary: The US president’s actions have inadvertently raised the appeal of renewable energy

items.title: Nestle India flags potential price hikes as West Asia war impacts costs
items.summary: Nestlé India chairman and managing director Manish Tiwary noted that his team is focused more on things that can be controlled, such as cost optimization, than worry about external headwinds/

items.title: Francisco Partners in talks to buy payments company Moneris
items.summary: Royal Bank of Canada and Bank of Montreal are latest North American banks trying to offload a payments processor

items.title: Voters will judge Trump on the economy - how is it doing?
items.summary: How much Donald Trump can do with the time he has left as president will depend massively on the cost of living.

items.title: Lodha Developers aims 2.5-fold jump in annual net profit to Rs 8,500 cr by FY31: MD Abhishek Lodha
items.summary: Lodha Developers plans a significant profit increase by fiscal 2031. The company targets over Rs 8,500 crore in net profit. This growth is fueled by strong demand for homes and commercial properties. Lodha Developers has a robust development pipeline and a strong market position. The Indian housing market is expected to expand for decades.

items.title: Cement cos in Meghalaya imported nearly 3 lakh mt of coal without valid papers: HC-appointed panel
items.summary: Two cement companies in Meghalaya face accusations of violating norms in transporting over 2.93 lakh metric tonnes of coal. A high court-appointed committee reported the companies moved the coal without mandatory approvals. This movement also lacked essential documents and weekly returns. The committee recommended strict enforcement and improved tracking systems to curb illegal coal movement across the state.

items.title: Airlines can cancel flights in advance over fuel shortages under new plans
items.summary: Ministers hope move would help avoid last-minute flight cancellations for passengers this summer over Middle East fuel supplies.

items.title: Vedanta is changing its policy that assured at least 30% profit gets distributed as dividend
items.summary: Vedanta is replacing its mandatory 30% profit-distribution rule with a discretionary, board-driven dividend policy as it prepares to split into five entities, eliminating the predictable yield that attracted income-focused investors.

items.title: Will the West Asia war accelerate the age of electricity? Explained in charts
items.summary: The 1973 oil crisis dramatically changed the way that economies used fossil fuels such as petroleum. The ongoing US-Iran war could also reshape energy usage—for good.

items.title: Global week ahead: Is 'Sell in May' just a myth?
items.summary: Should investors 'sell in May' or flip a coin?

items.title: Detroit carmakers warn of $5bn commodities shock due to Iran war
items.summary: Sector faces rising prices for supplies from aluminium to plastics and paint

items.title: Investors pile into clean power as Iran war drives push for energy security
items.summary: Renewables funds attract biggest flows for 5 years, as focus shifts from climate change to geopolitical crisis

items.title: Private equity investor body sounds alarm on ‘conflict vehicles’
items.summary: Continuation vehicles and rush for retail money are driving problematic behaviour by industry, warns ILPA executive

items.title: Software group IFS gears up for listing amid AI unease
items.summary: CEO Mark Moffat says industrial software group will consider London, Europe and New York for IPO

items.title: Warren Buffett tells Tim Cook to 'take a bow' as Apple CEO gets surprise send-off at Berkshire annual meeting
items.summary: During Berkshire Hathaway's meeting, Warren Buffett praised Tim Cook's pivotal role in Apple's rise. He highlighted how the  $35 billion investment by him in the company is now worth $185 billion.

items.title: Struggling High Streets fuel sense of neglect for voters ahead of local elections
items.summary: Failing High Streets fuel a wider sense of political discontent which could prove crucial in the upcoming elections for English councils in May.

items.title: Nvidia is losing ground as world's most valuable company, says report. Who can be the first to hit $6 trillion?
items.summary: Nvidia has held the title of the world's most valuable company, but Google parent Alphabet is closing in fast. Here is why the crown could change hands within weeks, according to Barron's.

items.title: CEA Nageswaran calls for capex push amid EV growth momentum
items.summary: India's top companies saw profits soar post-Covid. However, investments lagged. Chief economic adviser V Anantha Nageswaran called for corporate introspection. He highlighted that companies accumulated cash instead of investing in real assets. This forced the public sector to drive growth. Nageswaran stressed the need for private sector investment to seize domestic opportunities and address trade deficits.

items.title: Spirit Airlines shutting down after rescue talks collapse
items.summary: The airline had been in talks with the Trump administration about a $500m bailout.

items.title: Spirit Airlines shuts down after failing to reach a bailout deal, ending discount travel era
items.summary: Talks with bondholders for a government bailout this week failed to yield a deal.

items.title: CEO Greg Abel moves to assure Berkshire shareholders in a post-Buffett world, with record cash
items.summary: BERKSHIRE-AGM/ (WRAPUP 7, PIX):WRAPUP 7-CEO Greg Abel moves to assure Berkshire shareholders in a post-Buffett world, with record cash

items.title: Finance Ministry seeks faster disposal by DRTs, holds talks
items.summary: "Key areas of discussion included strengthening oversight and monitoring mechanisms within banks to enhance recoveries through DRTs, prioritising high-value cases for optimal recovery outcomes; leveraging Lok Adalats as an effective alternate dispute resolution," it said, adding mechanisms for expeditious disposal, procedural reforms to accelerate case disposal and comprehensive capacity-building initiatives were discussed.

items.title: NITI Aayog gets two new full-time members
items.summary: This takes the total number of members in the Aayog to seven, after Prime Minister Narendra Modi last month restructured the institution with the appointment of a new vice chairman and five full-time members. The PM is the chairman of the Aayog, which was constituted on January 1, 2015, in place of the Planning Commission.

items.title: Berkshire Meeting Highlights Tough Balancing Act for Greg Abel
items.summary: Greg Abel didn’t take long to address the elephant in the room.

items.title: Parliamentary Panels flag ministries’ budget gaps: Funds cut at RE stage, still unspent
items.summary: Parliamentary committees have flagged a consistent trend of central ministries failing to spend allocated budgets, leading to significant fund surrenders. Reports highlight poor budgeting and forecasting models across major ministries, including Defence, Petroleum, and Housing, with substantial reductions at the revised estimates stage and low utilization rates.

items.title: Plurilaterals without guardrails can fragment trading system: Experts
items.summary: Global trade volumes are growing despite concerns about the WTO. Experts discussed reforming the WTO's rules and governance. They highlighted the rise of 'precautionism' and the need for balanced reform. India's role in plurilateral negotiations was debated. The WTO remains indispensable for global trade and multilateralism.

items.title: Govt eases FDI norms under FEMA for up to 10% Chinese stake
items.summary: India has eased foreign investment rules. Overseas firms with up to ten percent Chinese ownership can now invest in India without prior government approval. This change applies to investments under the automatic route. The government aims to boost foreign capital inflows. This move follows earlier restrictions imposed in 2020. The new rules are effective immediately.

items.title: NHAI rolls out India's first stop-free toll system on NH-48 in Gujarat
items.summary: The new system removes physical toll barriers and enables seamless vehicle movement. "The MLFF framework introduces barrier-less tolling with minimal human intervention, enabling vehicles to pass through toll locations without stopping and ensuring seamless traffic flow," the release said.

items.title: Netweb Technologies Q4 results: Revenue leaps 90%, profit after tax jumps to ₹706 million, up 66% YoY — Key highlights
items.summary: Netweb Technologies, provider for AMD, Intel, Nvidia and Samsung, called FY26 a “landmark” year, with full-year revenue jumping 90% YoY to ₹21,836 million. PAT for the quarter stood at ₹706 million, representing 65.7% YoY growth.

items.title: France seeks enforceable rules in India-EU FTA talks
items.summary: In a move to strengthen economic ties, France is pushing for actionable measures to eliminate trade obstacles between India and the European Union. Minister Delegate Nicolas Forissier emphasized the urgency of addressing non-tariff challenges. With the recent Free Trade Agreement in place, the aim is to ensure it serves as a win-win for both regions.

items.title: DEA notifies FDI easing for foreign cos with up to 10 pc Chinese stake under FEMA
items.summary: New rules allow overseas firms with up to 10 percent Chinese ownership to invest in India automatically. This applies to sectors open for foreign direct investment. However, companies registered in China, Hong Kong, or countries sharing a land border with India are excluded. These changes aim to streamline investment while maintaining oversight. The Finance Ministry has notified these updated regulations.
